CPC Discussion topics.value: Skills disucussed for different career options, College interest | How to manage or control our online reputation | Getting into college and thinking about civic engagement opportunities | Learning how to set smalrt goals and various examples | resume writing, personal challenges for goal setting | Statements for growth mindsets and how they apply to personal challenges | Being proactive, setting goals and prioritizing | Introductions/getting to know each other | Why is goal setting important | How to set SMART goals | How to build confidence by practicing answers before hand, how to build a strategy for interviews, civic engagement on the importance of internships and other opportunities while at school | How to interact with the company throughout the interview process, how to have confident body language, how to answer tough questions, STAR answer method. | I asked each student common interview questions, we debriefed and allowed students to ask questions about interviewing and my role. | We discussed SMART goals, having a specific, measurable, actionable, realistic and time able goal plan. | Having a Plan, Making a List, Doing the Hard Stuff First, How to not Procrastinate, Focusing on one Task at a Time, How to Avoid Pitfalls | How to destress, common causes of stress at work, the negative impact they could have, how the intern can use things she enjoys to destress if needed | The Impact of Positive and Negative Customer Service, How Customer Service applies to any profession, what Customer Service Skills are important, Listening Skills | How to be specific, measurable, achievable, relevant and time focused  when setting goals | SMART Goals - Having a plan and being able to measure success | Keeping a positive mind, being organized, and having tools to eliminate stress | How to get rid of stress, and how to be more confident (Positive Thinking) | How to prepare, how to best answer the questions, and to come in with confidence.

CPC Why.value: The mentees are engaged and come ready to discuss. They're receptive of the lessons or skills I'm teaching them. | Mimi and Amizo were interactive and interested in the session. | The mentees were participating through the session. | The mentees' engagement in the session was encouraging | The interest of the interns | Good interaction with the interns. | The interns are open to learning | The interns' attention and interactions | The students were engaged in the discussion | Great first interaction with Intern, and helpful introduction at beginning of the call. | It took about 15 minutes for the student to arrive in the breakout room - unfortunately cut our time short. The call itself went great, intern comes across as very engaged and eager to learn. | We did a mock interview and both interns did a great job answering questions and participating. It was great to get to know them better while practicing interview skills. | Was unable to get in the meeting and unfortunatly ran 5 min late | Student was a great participant and is very engaged during the session. | Intern was a great participant and was very interactive. She gave input and approved our next topic. I want to ensure the meetings are of value to her and she shared that she has been able to learn from our meetings. | Intern was very engaged and provided great examples to all of my questions. | Students were both very engaged. | Joanna was very engaged, she took notes, asked questions and also opened up a lot more | Both students were very engaged, they are opening up and allowing me to get to know them better. | Students were great! Both had great participation | Students were on their way out of school and took the session from the bathroom. They are both such great personalities, but I dont think they are setting themselves up for success. They try hard and participate well, but there seems to be a lot of distractions which takes away from their true effort, which is there.
